sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items:
recording-releases — skip action button rendering when the page has no releases: on recording pages (e.g. `/recording/`) where the `

Appears on releases

` heading is immediately followed by a `

No releases found which feature this recording.

` paragraph, there is nothing to fetch or display. The script now detects this condition early — right after `pageType` and `headerContainer` are resolved and before any UI elements are created — and exits cleanly without rendering the `🧮 Show all Releases for Recording` button or any other action buttons. The detection walks forward siblings of the `h2.appears-on-releases` element (skipping blank text nodes) and checks whether the first element sibling is a `

` whose text starts with "no releases found" (case-insensitive). When matched, a descriptive message is written to the browser console via `console.log` (always) and `Lib.debug` (when debug logging is enabled), then the initializer returns immediately.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Auto-resize (↔️ Resize) — Event column still wrapping after v9.99.37 fix: v9.99.37 correctly eliminated the browser table-fixup problem (orphaned `<td>` causing anonymous table-row boxes with `white-space:normal`), but a second independent wrapping mechanism remained: MusicBrainz's own stylesheet sets `overflow-wrap:anywhere` directly on every `.wrap-anywhere` anchor element. CSS inheritance only fills property values that are not explicitly set — it does NOT override explicit stylesheet rules. Setting `overflow-wrap:normal` on the parent `measureDiv` therefore had no effect on descendant `<a class="wrap-anywhere">` nodes, which retained `overflow-wrap:anywhere` and continued to break text at arbitrary code points inside the measurement div, causing `offsetWidth` to reflect only the width of the wrapped first line rather than the full single-line content width. Fixed by traversing all cloned descendant elements with `measureDiv.querySelectorAll('*')` after they are appended and explicitly setting `el.style.whiteSpace = 'nowrap'`, `el.style.overflowWrap = 'normal'`, and `el.style.wordBreak = 'normal'` on each one. Inline styles always win over stylesheet rules regardless of specificity, guaranteeing that no descendant element can cause a line break during measurement. The same querySelectorAll pass is applied in both the header and data-cell measurement loops.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Auto-resize (↔️ Resize) — Event column cells containing a blank-icon/artwork-icon span not measured to their full single-line width: the column-width measurement loop cloned each `<td>` / `<th>` and appended it directly to a hidden `position:absolute` measurement `<div>`. Orphaned `<td>` / `<th>` elements appended outside a `<table>` trigger the browser's CSS table-fixup algorithm, which silently wraps them in anonymous table / table-row boxes. Those anonymous boxes carry the UA-stylesheet default of `white-space:normal`, overriding the `white-space:nowrap` that was set on the measurement div. As a result, content inside the cloned cell wrapped during measurement, and `offsetWidth` reflected only the widest non-breaking token (e.g. the fixed-pixel width of the `artwork-icon eaa-icon` span) rather than the full single-line width of icon + main-link text + comment. This made the Event column too narrow for cells that contained a long venue name followed by a `<span class="comment">` (e.g. `2025-10-18: David Geffen Theater … (5th Annual Academy Museum Gala)`) — the comment text wrapped to the next line. Fixed by cloning the element's *children* directly into the measurement div (via `Array.from(cell.childNodes).forEach(n => measureDiv.appendChild(n.cloneNode(true)))`) rather than the `<td>` / `<th>` itself, eliminating the table-fixup entirely. Additionally added `word-break:normal` and `overflow-wrap:normal` to the measurement div's `cssText` so that the `.wrap-anywhere` class (`overflow-wrap:anywhere`) present on event-link anchors cannot cause mid-word breaks even in the non-table measurement context.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Unique-values dropdown — horizontal scroll position lost after selecting a value: clicking an item in the "Show unique column values" dropdown (📊 button in a column header) triggered `applyUniqVal()` which dispatched a synthetic `input` event and caused `runFilter()` to re-render the table. `runFilter()` saved and restored the vertical scroll position (`window.scrollY`) to prevent the page jumping, but the `window.scrollTo()` call at the end of the function hardcoded `0` as the horizontal argument — `window.scrollTo(0, __scrollY)` — unconditionally resetting horizontal scroll to the left edge on every filter run. When the table had been auto-resized via the ↔️ Resize button and the user had scrolled right to a column not visible in the default viewport (e.g. the "Comment" column), selecting any unique value from the dropdown snapped the viewport back to the leftmost position. Fixed by also capturing `window.scrollX` at the start of `runFilter()` (alongside the existing `__scrollY`) and restoring both axes: `window.scrollTo(__scrollX, __scrollY)`.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: event-aliases (Bug 1) — controls container inserted before disambiguation span: on entity alias pages (and any other page where the h1's `headerContainer` resolves to the inner `<a>` element rather than the `<h1>` itself), the controls `<div>` was inserted via `headerContainer.after(controlsContainer)` — immediately after the `<a>` link, pushing any pre-existing h1 siblings that followed the `<a>` (the date text node and a plain `<span class="comment">` disambiguation such as "(Bleachers)") to appear after the action buttons. `relocateH1CommentSpan()` then skipped the plain span (no `<i title>` inside) and left it stranded after the controls. Fixed by resolving the owning `<h1>` via `headerContainer.closest('h1')` and calling `h1El.appendChild(controlsContainer)` instead, which appends the controls at the end of the h1 so that any pre-existing text and comment siblings remain in their natural position before the buttons. A safe fallback to the previous `after()` behaviour is retained when no parent `<h1>` is found.

All *-aliases pages (Bug 2) — Locale column incorrectly split into MB-Name / Comment: `extractMainColumn: 'Locale'` was set on all 11 aliases page definitions (`instrument-aliases`, `area-aliases`, `place-aliases`, `series-aliases`, `label-aliases`, `work-aliases`, `artist-aliases`, `releasegroup-aliases`, `release-aliases`, `recording-aliases`, `event-aliases`). The MB-Name/Comment extraction pipeline then treated the Locale cell (e.g. `English United States`) as the main entity column — since the cell contains no `` link, the fallback extracted the first text node as MB-Name and the `primary` role indicator as Comment, producing wrong synthetic columns: MB-Name = "English United States", Comment = "primary". The Locale column on alias tables is purely descriptive plain text; it never contains entity links or entity disambiguation comments. Fixed by removing `extractMainColumn: 'Locale'` (and the surrounding `features` block where it was the sole entry) from all 11 aliases page definitions. The commented-out lines and an explanatory note are left in place for future reference.

sections.label: 📝 Documentation
items: HELP file updated to reflect the current state of the userscript (v9.99.33) and VZ_MBLibrary (v3.14.3). New and expanded sections: Unique-Values Column Dropdown (📊 button, quickfilter bar, count badges, keyboard navigation, Escape behaviour); Load-from-Disk three-phase dialog (Load → Filter → Render with Alt+L / Alt+F / Alt+R shortcuts); Filter Input States & Border Colors (idle / active / error states, ⋮ resizable handle for global and sub-table inputs); Cross-tag filter highlighting note added to the Filtering section; Synthetic Columns table expanded to include Place/Area/Country (from Location), MB-Area/Country (from Area), Format Types, and MB-Name/Comment; H1 Alias Line section describing the relocateH1CommentSpan() behaviour on Release Group and Event pages; Load Dialog Keyboard Shortcuts section; Settings section expanded with all new setting groups and entries (filter border colors, unique-values badge colors, sort progress threshold, render warning threshold, download notification font size, sub-table filter border colors, overflow tables in new tab); Changelog Dialog section updated to document live search with highlighting and ✕ clear button; Library Settings section added (🔧 Library Settings GM menu item, font size/family options); Multi-table Pages section updated with sub-table filter error reporting and overflow-tab setting; SAVE & LOAD section updated with informative filename format.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Load-from-Disk dialog — history dropdown regression fixed: the 3-phase dialog rewrite introduced a regression where the history toggle button (▼) was invisible for the entire dialog session whenever the GM history store was empty at open-time. Root cause: the toggle's `display` was baked into the static template HTML (`display:${history.length > 0 ? 'inline-block' : 'none'}`) and never updated afterwards. As a consequence, the very first time a user typed a filter expression and clicked “Filter Data”, that expression was correctly saved to GM storage — but the toggle stayed hidden and the dropdown was never refreshed, making the feature appear entirely absent. Fixed by (1) extracting a `refreshHistoryDropdown()` local helper that rebuilds the dropdown's `innerHTML`, re-attaches item click-handlers, and syncs the toggle's `display` to `history.length`; (2) calling it once at dialog-setup time (superseding the static `historyItemsHTML` in the template); and (3) calling it again inside `filterBtn.onclick` immediately after `history = updated` so the toggle and item list stay in sync within the same dialog session. Additionally, `el.textContent.trim()` is now used when populating the filter input from a history item to guard against any leading/trailing whitespace in the rendered item text.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: artist-releasegroups — MB-Name / Comment extraction was silently skipped: the `artist-releasegroups` pageType uses a dedicated fetch branch (iterating `doc.querySelectorAll('table.tbl')`) that was completely separate from the generic `else`-branch where the MB-Name / Comment extraction pipeline lives. Consequently, setting `features: { extractMainColumn: 'Title' }` on the page definition caused the synthetic column *headers* to be injected correctly (that path reads `activeDefinition.features` before the branch split) but the synthetic column *cells* were never populated — the rows were cloned with `document.importNode` and pushed without appending `tdName` / `tdComment`. A second, compounding bug was an explicit guard `if (pageType !== 'artist-releasegroups')` in the else-branch that also suppressed appending those cells. Both issues are now fixed: (1) The `artist-releasegroups` branch received a full copy of the shared synthetic-column pipeline — MB-Name / Comment extraction (steps 1–2), active column extractor evaluation (step 2), excluded-column deletion (step 3), synthetic-cell appending (step 4), and conditional tdName / tdComment appending (step 5). (2) The stale `pageType !== 'artist-releasegroups'` guard in the else-branch was replaced by `if (mainColIdx !== -1)`, making the guard data-driven (does `extractMainColumn` resolve to a valid index?) rather than hardcoded to a single page type.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: H1 comment-span relocation (complex case) — the `relocateH1CommentSpan()` IIFE now handles the more complex structure found on event pages where the `` inside `` contains both an `ALIAS` element *and* trailing sibling text (extra show title) after the closing ``: e.g. `Southside Johnny & The Asbury Jukes at The Stone Pony, Southside Johnny & The Asbury Jukes Stone Pony 25th Anniversary Show`. Previously the entire span was removed and only ALIAS was surfaced, silently discarding the extra title. Now: (1) ALIAS is relocated to the alias line below h1 as before. (2) The extra sibling text (all text/element nodes following `` within the ``, or the `commentSpan` when no `` wrapper is present) is collected, its leading `", "` stripped, and re-injected into the h1 as a new `(EXTRA)` so it remains visible on the title line. When the extra text is blank after trimming the span is removed entirely (simple-case behaviour unchanged). Additionally, the early-return guard was tightened: spans without an `` element (plain MB disambiguation comments such as "(live)") are now explicitly skipped before any mutation occurs.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items:
H1 comment-span relocation — on release-group pages MusicBrainz may inject a `( … )` directly inside the h1, placing the alias text (with surrounding parentheses) on the same line as the entity title and the userscript action buttons. The new `relocateH1CommentSpan()` IIFE removes that span from the h1, extracts the alias label from the `` attribute (e.g. "Primary alias") and the alias text from the element's text content, then injects a replacement `

` styled in italics immediately above `

` (or directly after the h1 when no subheader is found). The rendered line reads «Primary alias: Bruce Springsteen & The E Street Band at Athletic & Convocation Center», matching the requested layout: title line → alias line → subheader line. The injected element is removed and re-created on soft-navigation reloads to stay idempotent.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: saveTableDataToDisk filename — two glitches fixed: (1) `-filtered` suffix: the internal pageType for `link_type_id` pages (e.g. `artist-relationships-filtered`) included the `-filtered` marker in the filename even though the detail slug already identifies the relationship. Fixed by deriving `pageTypeSlug = pageType.replace(/-filtered$/, '')` and using that in the filename instead of the raw `pageType`. (2) Double underscores in detail slug: special characters such as `℗` (and their surrounding spaces) are converted to underscores then stripped, leaving consecutive underscores like `holds_phonographic_copyright__for`. Fixed by appending `.replace(/_+/g, '_')` as a final step in the `toSlug` helper. Example before: `MB-artist-relationships-filtered-holds_phonographic_copyright__for-87-….json.gz`. Example after: `MB-artist-relationships-holds_phonographic_copyright_for-87-….json.gz`.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: saveTableDataToDisk — informative filename format: the generated filename now follows `MB-<pageType>[-<detail>-]<rowCount>-<timestamp>.json.gz` instead of the bare `mb-<pageType>-<timestamp>.json.gz`. The optional `<detail>` slug is derived from the page context via a shared `toSlug()` helper (lowercase → whitespace to underscores → strip non-alphanumeric): (a) Multi-button pages (e.g. artist-releases with multiple fetch buttons): the label of the last clicked fetch button is slugified — tracked by the new module-level `lastClickedButtonLabel` variable which is written in `startFetchingProcess` alongside the `activeDefinition` update. (b) Filtered-relationship pages whose URL contains `?…link_type_id=…`: the quoted text inside the first `#content h2` (matching straight or curly quotes) is slugified — e.g. `"holds phonographic copyright (℗) for" relationships` → `holds_phonographic_copyright_for`. (c) All other pages: no detail segment. Examples: `MB-artist-releases-official_rgs-342-2026-03-04T12-00-00.json.gz`, `MB-artist-relationships-holds_phonographic_copyright_for-87-2026-03-04T12-00-00.json.gz`, `MB-artist-recordings-1204-2026-03-04T12-00-00.json.gz`.

showDownloadNotification — configurable message font size: a new `sa_ui_download_notification_font_size` setting (type `text`, default `1em`) has been added to the `configSchema` UI APPEARANCE section. The value is applied as `msg.style.fontSize` on the message `<div>` inside the download notification popup, making it independently adjustable from the rest of the popup UI (e.g. `0.85em`, `14px`, `1.1rem`).

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Page-type mismatch warning broken after 'Load from Disk' dialog rewrite — the pageType mismatch check in `loadTableDataFromDisk` referenced `loadFromDiskBtn` (wrong anchor for `Lib.showCustomConfirm`) and `fileInput` (the outer hidden input, not the dialog's `dialogFileInput`), so cancelling the confirm had no effect: the dialog stayed open with `rawData`/`rawFile` already set and the user could still proceed to render. Fixed by moving the check into `showLoadFilterDialog`'s Phase 1 handler (`dialogFileInput.onchange`), immediately after the required-fields validation. On cancel the handler now resets `rawData`/`rawFile` to null, restores the Load button, displays a cancellation message in `loadStatus`, and returns without revealing Phase 2. The dead check block in `loadTableDataFromDisk` was replaced with a comment noting the moved location.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Header-text cleaning — all `replace(/[⇅▲▼]/g, '')` call sites now also strip the `📊` unique-values button emoji from column header text before comparison or export. Affected sites: CSV export header scan (line ~2890), JSON export header scan (~2998), Org-Mode export header scan (~3068), synthetic-column existence check (~9133), MB-Name column existence check (~9151), single-table sort column-name derivation (~2535), multi-table sort column-name derivation (~2042, which also strips superscript digits), and the sort-state persistence column-name read (~8661, unicode-range form). The two call sites that already included `📊` (getCleanColName at ~8983 and the stats-panel path at ~11971) were correct and left unchanged.

Sub-table filter status — singular 'row' when exactly one row matches: `✓ Filtered 1 rows […]` now correctly reads `✓ Filtered 1 row […]`. Implemented via a ternary `${rowsInTable === 1 ? 'row' : 'rows'}` at the single affected call site (~8986).

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Save-to-Disk filename disambiguation: the generated filename now includes an extra slug between the `mb-${pageType}` prefix and the timestamp so that files saved from different button contexts or query-string variants of the same page type are immediately distinguishable without opening them. Two cases are handled: (1) Multi-button pages (e.g. artist-releases with 'Official RGs', 'Non-official RGs', 'Official VA RGs', 'Non-official VA RGs' buttons): when `activeDefinition.buttons.length > 1` and `activeDefinition.label` is set, the clicked button's label is lowercased, spaces are converted to underscores, and all non-alphanumeric characters are stripped to form the suffix (e.g. `mb-artist-releases-official_rgs-2026-03-04T…json.gz`). (2) Query-string pages filtered by `link_type_id` (e.g. `/area/…/releases?direction=1&link_type_id=835`): the text content of the first `#content h3` element is lowercased and slug-converted to form the suffix (e.g. `mb-area-releases-recorded_at-2026-03-04T…json.gz`). Pages that match neither condition continue to use the original `mb-${pageType}-${timestamp}.json.gz` format.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: normalizeExtractedText — Step 3: remove spurious space before comma separators between list items. MusicBrainz area, label, and catalog-number columns render multiple values separated by a bare comma+newline (e.g. '<bdi>Honolulu\n</bdi>,\n<a …>') which, after the existing join-and-collapse pipeline, produced an artificial space before the comma: 'Honolulu , Hawaii'. This caused both column-filter matching and highlight-span placement to diverge from what the browser renders ('Honolulu, Hawaii'), so a user typing 'Honolulu, Hawaii' would get no match and no highlighting even though the row was visually correct. Fixed by appending '.replace(/ ,/g, ",")' as a new Step 3 in normalizeExtractedText, applied after whitespace collapse (Step 1) and bracket-interior-space removal (Step 2). The fix is safe because no MusicBrainz data format uses an intentional space before a comma. All four affected column types confirmed: area lists ('Honolulu, Hawaii, United States'), comment-disambiguated items ('Ithaca (city), New York'), multi-label columns ('Columbia (…), Sony BMG Music Entertainment (…)'), and catalog-number columns ('CBS S 65480, S 65480'). The stale orphaned JSDoc stub ('Helper to get visible text only…') that preceded the real block was also removed.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Unique-values dropdown — count badge alignment: all '(n)' badges within a single column dropdown now share a uniform width derived from the digit count of the largest occurrence count in that column. Badges are rendered in a monospace font with right-aligned text so the numbers line up vertically regardless of their digit count (e.g. '   (12)' above '(21345)'). The width is computed per column open via 'maxDigits = String(maxCount).length' and 'badgeChWidth = maxDigits + 2' (adding the two parentheses), applied as 'min-width: Nch' on the badge span. The previous fixed 'min-width: 1.6em' and 'text-align: center' are replaced by the dynamic ch-unit width with 'text-align: right'. The 'font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums' property (redundant with monospace) has been removed.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Unique-values dropdown — occurrence count badge: each entry in the unique-values dropdown now shows a styled '(n)' badge to its left indicating how many currently visible rows contain that value. The badge is purely presentational (aria-hidden); clicking an entry or pressing Enter copies only the raw column value into the filter field, exactly as before. The badge colors are configurable via two new settings: 'sa_uniq_count_color' (text color, default dark-black #111111) and 'sa_uniq_count_bg' (background color, default light-yellow #fffacd), both exposed as color pickers in the FILTER SETTINGS section of the configuration UI.

Unique-values dropdown — internal value tracking changed from a Set to a Map so that each distinct value's occurrence count is recorded in a single pass over the visible tbody rows, with no additional DOM traversal overhead.

Unique-values dropdown — 'renderItems' updated: the non-filter branch now uses 'item.appendChild(document.createTextNode(v))' instead of 'item.textContent = v' so that the count badge node that was already appended to the item is preserved when the item is rendered without an active quickfilter string.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Filter highlighting — cross-tag match highlighting now works for ALL three filter levels (global, sub-table, and column). The `highlightText` function (used by global and column filters) and the inline highlighting block inside `applySubFilter` (used by sub-table filters) both previously used a per-text-node TreeWalker approach that silently produced no highlight span for any search term whose characters spanned an inline element boundary (e.g. `<a>`, `<bdi>`, `<span>`). Both are now replaced by calls to the new shared `highlightCrossTag(root, regex, className)` helper introduced in this release.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Filter highlighting — new `highlightCrossTag(root, regex, className)` helper: concatenates the plain text of all text nodes within `root`, finds all regex matches in the concatenated string, then replaces each affected text node with a DocumentFragment whose segments are either plain `Text` nodes (outside matches) or `<span class="mb-*-highlight">` elements (inside matches). Text nodes are processed in reverse document order so that earlier nodes' DOM positions are never invalidated by later mutations. Shared by `highlightText` (global and column filters) and `applySubFilter` (sub-table filters).

Filter highlighting — cross-tag match highlighting: the `highlightText` function has been replaced by a new two-function pair (`highlightText` + `highlightCrossTag`). The new implementation concatenates the plain text of all text nodes within each `<td>`, finds all regex matches in the concatenated string, and then replaces each affected text node with a DocumentFragment whose segments are either plain `Text` nodes (outside matches) or `<span class="mb-*-highlight">` elements (inside matches). This correctly highlights search terms that span across multiple sibling or nested inline elements (e.g. `<a>`, `<bdi>`, `<span>`) — previously such cross-tag matches produced no visible highlight even though the row was correctly shown/hidden.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Load-from-Disk dialog — completely redesigned as a three-phase workflow: (1) Load: the user picks a file which is read and parsed entirely into memory with no filter applied; a spinner appears on the button during reading and a '✅ Loaded N rows from filename' status line is shown below the buttons when done. (2) Filter: revealed only after a successful load; the user may optionally enter a filter expression (with Case Sensitive / Regular Expression / Exclude Matches toggles); clicking 'Filter Data' counts matching rows in memory without touching the DOM and shows the count ('N of T rows match → will be rendered' or 'N rows will be rendered (M excluded)') below the button. (3) Render: revealed only when the filter count is > 0; clicking 'Render Data' calls the existing loadTableDataFromDisk() with the cached File object and the chosen filter parameters, shows a 'Rendering…' spinner, and closes the dialog when rendering completes. The dialog creates its own private file input so it is completely decoupled from the always-visible prefilter bar. Alt+L / Alt+F / Alt+R keyboard shortcuts activate each phase button respectively; Escape dismisses the dialog at any phase.
Load-from-Disk dialog — in-memory row counting for the Filter phase uses a lightweight HTML-strip helper (regex, no DOM construction) making it fast even for large datasets.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Unique-values column dropdown — quickfilter bar: a sticky 'Quick filter…' input field is now rendered at the top of every unique-values panel (the 📊 dropdown). The field is auto-focused when the panel opens so the user can type immediately. As the user types, the item list is narrowed in real time to only show values whose text contains the filter string (case-insensitive). The matching substring is highlighted with configurable foreground and background colors (new settings: 'Unique-Values Quickfilter Highlight Color' and 'Unique-Values Quickfilter Highlight Background' in the 🎨 Filter Highlight Colors section of Settings). A clickable '×' button appears (fades in) to the right of the input field when there is text; clicking it clears the filter and restores all items, keeping focus in the input. Pressing Escape once clears the filter (if non-empty); pressing Escape a second tgime (empty field) closes the panel. Arrow keys / Home / End / Enter continue to navigate and select the currently visible (filtered) items.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items:
Filtering — cross-tag text matching now works correctly: filter strings that span multiple inline HTML elements (e.g. 'Bruce Springsteen & The E Street Band' spread across two <a> elements and a bare text node) previously produced no results because getCleanColumnText / getCleanVisibleText joined the raw text-node values — each carrying its own trailing newlines and indentation — with a plain space, yielding a string such as 'Bruce Springsteen
    &
    The E Street Band' that never matched the user query. Fixed by appending .replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim() to the joined result in both helpers so all whitespace runs (including 
, 	, and multi-space indentation) collapse to a single space before any comparison. The HTML structure is left completely intact for display/rendering purposes; only the string used for the match/highlight test is normalised.

Unique-values column dropdown — script-tag JSON blobs (e.g. release-events and author-roles data embedded by MusicBrainz as <script type='application/json'> inside table cells) are now excluded from the dropdown: openUniqDrop previously used cell.textContent.trim() which includes the raw JSON. It now calls getCleanColumnText() which already skips <script> subtrees and also returns whitespace-normalised text, giving cleaner and shorter display labels consistent with what the filter input matches against.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Prevent unicodechar table popup when pressing Ctrl-M in text fields.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Unique-values dropdown — Enter key now selects the keyboard-focused item: the original keydown listener on the 📊 button (attached at element-creation time) was firing before the per-invocation `kh` handler (registered inside openUniqDrop). When Enter was pressed with the panel open and an item highlighted via ArrowDown/ArrowUp, the original listener called openUniqDrop() which toggled the panel closed and cleared _uniqDropOwner, so kh saw no owner and returned early without applying the value. Fixed by guarding the original listener: if ev.key === 'Enter' and the panel is already open for this button (_uniqDropOwner === uniqBtn), the original listener returns immediately and lets kh handle selection.

Column header rendering — ▼ and 📊 no longer overlap on narrow columns (e.g. 'Year'): the previous float:right layout for the 📊 button did not widen the table cell, so the sort icons and 📊 overlapped on short column names. Replaced the float with a .mb-col-hdr-flex (display:flex; white-space:nowrap; width:100%) wrapper div that contains ⇅ colName ▲▼ 📊 in order; the 📊 button uses margin-left:auto to push itself to the right edge, and the flex container forces the column wide enough to show all symbols without overlap.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Unique-values column dropdown: a right-aligned '📊' button is now added to every column header cell by makeTableSortableUnified. Clicking it (or pressing Enter/Space while it has focus) opens a fixed-position scrollable panel (#mb-col-uniq-dropdown) listing all distinct non-empty values present in the column's currently visible tbody rows, sorted case-insensitively. Selecting a value (mouse click or keyboard Enter) writes it into the column's mb-col-filter-input and fires an immediate filter pass via a synthetic input event. The panel is a DOM singleton created lazily on first use.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Unique-values dropdown — mouse-wheel scroll no longer closes the panel: the global scroll close-listener now checks ev.target and skips closing when the scroll originates inside the dropdown element itself.

Unique-values dropdown — ArrowDown/ArrowUp now scroll through the full list: moveFocus() now directly manipulates drop.scrollTop to keep the focused item visible within the panel's own overflow container instead of calling el.scrollIntoView(), which was scrolling the page rather than the panel and caused navigation to stall at the last initially-visible item.

Unique-values dropdown — re-render safety: '📊' is stripped from colName (in headers.forEach) and from getCleanColName() so repeated calls to makeTableSortableUnified do not accumulate the emoji in column labels.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fix ghost sidebar toggle handle: The sidebar toggle handle is now positioned dynamically via JS (updateHandlePosition) instead of relying on a hard-coded CSS 'right: 240px'. The root cause was that toggleAutoResizeColumns changes the sidebar from its default right-floated layout to 'position: sticky; left: 0', making the sidebar appear at the LEFT viewport edge during horizontal scrolling — so the fixed handle at 'right: 240px' ended up floating over the content area rather than being adjacent to the sidebar. updateHandlePosition() reads the sidebar's live getBoundingClientRect() on every scroll, resize, and toggle event and sets handle.style.right = (window.innerWidth - sidebar.left) px, keeping the handle flush against the sidebar's actual rendered left boundary at all times regardless of scroll position or CSS position changes. The CSS 'transition: right 0.3s ease' and the '.handle-collapsed { right: 0 !important }' rule have been removed to avoid CSS/JS fights on high-frequency scroll events.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Global filter: the 'sa_filter_focus_prefix' (default '🔍 ') is now permanently visible in the global filter field at all times — focused or not, empty or not. The field is initialized with the prefix on creation and every programmatic reset (fresh fetch, disk load, clear-all, ✕ button) restores it to prefix-only rather than to an empty string. This is different from column filter inputs, where the prefix appears only while the field has focus. All values read from the global filter field pass through stripFilterPrefix() before use, so the invariant is transparent to all filtering logic.

sections.label: ♻️ Refactoring
items: The focus and blur event listeners on the global filter input have been removed. The blur listener's secondary responsibility (calling runFilter() after stripping the prefix) is no longer needed because the prefix is never stripped on blur. The keydown guard (guardFilterPrefixKeydown) and the prefix-safety-net inside the input listener remain.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Setting 'sa_col_filter_focus_prefix' renamed to 'sa_filter_focus_prefix'. The decorative search-icon prefix now applies to the global filter field as well as column filter fields. Both types of filter input show the prefix while focused and strip it before using the value as a filter query.

Global filter: the field now gains the same focus-mode behaviour as column filters — the configured prefix (default '🔍 ') is prepended to the placeholder (or to the value if a filter string is already present) on focus, and stripped on blur. The keydown guard prevents Backspace/Delete/ArrowLeft/Home/Ctrl+A from reaching the prefix characters. Border colour continues to signal active state (no persistent background tint on blur, unlike column filters).
Escape key on the global filter now behaves like on column filters: first press retains the prefix and clears only the user-entered text; second press blurs the field.

Ctrl+G (focus global filter shortcut) now positions the cursor at the end of the editable content after the prefix, instead of calling select() which would have included the prefix characters in the selection.
The '✕' clear button for the global filter now resets the placeholder to the focus-state variant (prefix + base text) when the field remains focused after clearing.

sections.label: ♻️ Refactoring
items: The four column-filter-specific helpers (stripColFilterPrefix, applyColFilterFocusStyle, removeColFilterFocusStyle, guardColFilterPrefixKeydown) have been replaced by four generalized equivalents: getFilterFocusPrefix(), stripFilterPrefix(value), applyFilterFocusStyle(input, blurPlaceholder, focusBg?), removeFilterFocusStyle(input, idlePlaceholder, hasActiveBg?), and guardFilterPrefixKeydown(input, e). The old names are retained as deprecated const aliases so no existing call site breaks. All internal call sites have been updated to the new names.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Load from Disk — RegExp pre-filter now correctly applied: when the 'Regular Expression' checkbox in the Load dialog was checked, no rows were loaded at all. Root cause: confirmLoad() tried to sync the regex flag to the live filter bar by writing to 'preFilterRegexLabel.querySelector("input")' — but 'preFilterRegexLabel' was never declared anywhere in the script, so the typeof guard suppressed the write and 'preFilterRxCheckbox.checked' stayed false. The fileInput.onchange handler therefore always read isRegExp=false, fell into the plain-text path, and treated the pattern literally (e.g. '^Thunder' as the string '^thunder'), which matched nothing. Fixed: the dead reference to 'preFilterRegexLabel' is replaced with a direct 'preFilterRxCheckbox.checked = useRegex' assignment.

Ctrl+Shift+Esc shortcut for 'Clear ALL filters' removed: this key combination is intercepted at the OS level on most platforms (Windows opens Task Manager; some Linux desktop environments also handle it), so it never reliably reached the browser. The keydown handler block has been removed entirely. The 'Clear ALL filters' action remains available via the configurable 'sa_shortcut_clear_filters' shortcut (default: Ctrl+Shift+G) and by clicking the '✕ Clear ALL filters' button. The button tooltip now dynamically displays the currently configured shortcut key.

sections.label: 📝 Documentation
items: ShowAllEntityData_HELP.txt: removed all references to Ctrl+Shift+Esc from the FILTERING and KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS sections. The 'Clear all filters' entry in KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS now notes that the shortcut is configurable and that the button tooltip reflects the currently configured key.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Multi-table mode: the global '[x] Rx' checkbox now exclusively controls the global filter string. Previously its state was also forwarded to getColFilters() for every sub-table, which caused column-level filters to behave as regular expressions even when no sub-table Rx checkbox was checked. Fixed: in the groupedRows loop inside runFilter() the code now looks up the sub-table's own Rx checkbox (the input ending in '-rx-checkbox' inside .mb-subtable-filter-container of the preceding h3) and passes its state — not the global isRegExp flag — to getColFilters().

Multi-table mode: the sub-table '[x] Rx' checkbox now correctly governs BOTH the sub-table filter string AND the column-level filters of that sub-table. Previously column filters always received the global isRegExp flag regardless of the sub-table checkbox; now they receive the sub-table checkbox state.

Single-table mode: when '[x] Rx' is set and a column-level filter field contains an invalid regular expression, the error message is now correctly displayed in the '#mb-filter-status-display' span. Previously the error string was captured in a 'const' variable declared inside the else-branch of runFilter(), making it inaccessible in the status-update block that follows outside that branch (a block-scoping bug). Fixed: the variable is now declared as 'let _singleColRxErrors = []' before the if/else, and assigned (not re-declared) inside the else-branch, so the status-update block can always read it.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Multi-table pages: column-level regexp filters now match rows correctly. Previously the raw pattern was unconditionally lowercased before being stored in the colFilters array. In regexp mode the pattern must remain as-is — the 'i' flag in the matching step handles case-insensitivity, and lowercasing destroys character-class semantics (e.g. [A-Z] became [a-z]). Fixed: val is now set to raw when isRegExp is true.

Multi-table pages: the column regexp-error message written by getColFilters to a sub-table's span.mb-filter-status was being immediately overwritten by the post-filter status-update loop with a '✓ Filtered N rows' message. Fixed: the update loop now skips any status span that carries the colRxError marker set by getColFilters.

Single-table pages: column regexp-error messages were silently discarded because getTableFilterStatusSpan() returns null when there is no h3 before the table, so the if(statusSpan) guard suppressed the write. Additionally, the normal '✓ N rows' update would have overwritten any message anyway. Fixed: getColFilters now also populates result._rxErrors (an array of formatted error strings). In single-table mode the call site captures this array and, in the status-update block, shows the first error in filterStatusDisplay instead of the success message.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Sub-table filter border colors are now configurable: 'sa_subtable_filter_border_idle' (idle/empty, default #cccccc) and 'sa_subtable_filter_border_active' (active, default #008000). The ✕ and ⋮ edge strips update in sync via the new setSubTableFilterBorder() helper.

The error border-color setting for invalid regexp is now shared across all filter types, renamed from 'sa_global_filter_border_error' to 'sa_filter_border_error'. Applies to global, sub-table, and column-level filters.
Error border width increased from 2 px to 4 px in error state, ensuring the error color is clearly visible.
Sub-table filter regexp errors now write '⚠ Invalid regexp: …' to the span.mb-filter-status of the relevant sub-table, cleared when the pattern becomes valid.

Column-level filter regexp errors (when Rx is active) now write '⚠ Column "…": invalid regexp: …' to the span.mb-filter-status of the associated sub-table and apply the 4 px error border to the specific column input. Invalid patterns are skipped from the filter pass. New helper getTableFilterStatusSpan() centralises the h3-to-status-span lookup.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: stfInput border reset in 'Clear all column filters' now uses settings-driven stfBorderIdle()/stfBorderActive() instead of hardcoded rgb(204,204,204).

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Global filter border color now transitions through three configurable states: idle (empty field, default black), active (has valid filter text, default orange), and error (invalid regexp, default dark red). The border of the adjacent ✕ and ⋮ edge strips updates in sync. Three new color-picker settings — 'Global filter border — idle', 'Global filter border — active', 'Global filter border — error' — replace the single previous 'Global filter border color' setting.

When the Rx checkbox is active and the typed pattern is not a valid regular expression, an error message ('⚠ Invalid regexp: …') is shown immediately in the filter-status display in the error color, and the filter pass is skipped until the pattern becomes valid. The error message clears automatically once the regexp parses successfully.
Global filter box-shadow glow now uses the configured active border color instead of a hardcoded red.

Column filter placeholder is now visible on a freshly focused, empty field: the placeholder embeds the search-icon prefix (sa_col_filter_focus_prefix) followed by 'Filter on this column…', so the hint is readable as soon as focus arrives. The prefix is only moved into the field value when the user begins typing, preserving all prefix-guard behaviour for non-empty fields. On blur the placeholder reverts to '…'.

Global and sub-table filter fields are now resizable: a narrow ⋮ drag handle appears to the right of the ✕ clear button. Dragging it horizontally adjusts the input width (minimum 60 px). The ✕ button and ⋮ handle are distinct rectangular strips that share the input border color, eliminating any overlap between the clear action and the resize gesture.
Global filter placeholder reads '🔍 Global Filter… works across all sub-tables' on multi-table pages and '🔍 Global Filter…' on single-table pages.
Sub-table filter placeholder reads 'Filter "<name>"… just in this sub-table'.
New number settings 'Global filter initial width' (default 500 px) and 'Sub-table filter initial width' (default 320 px) control the starting width of the respective resizable filter fields.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Global filter border color is now configurable (single setting, default orange). Previously hardcoded to #ccc / red.
Global filter placeholder changed from '🔍 Global Filter...' to '🔍 Global Filter…' (ellipsis U+2026).
Sub-table filter placeholder changed from 'Filter "<name>"…' to 'Filter "<name>"… just in this sub-table'.
Column filter default (unfocused) placeholder changed from '...' to '…' (ellipsis U+2026).
Column filter focused placeholder changed to 'Filter on this column…'.
Global and sub-table filter fields made resizable via a dedicated ⋮ drag handle; initial widths configurable via sa_global_filter_initial_width and sa_subtable_filter_initial_width settings.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: RegExp pre-filter validation in the Load dialog was never reached: the invalid-pattern error was caught inside loadTableDataFromDisk(), which only runs after the dialog has already closed and the OS file-picker has opened, giving the user no way to correct the pattern. Fixed by moving validation into confirmLoad() (now async) where it fires before closeDialog() and fileInput.click(). On a bad pattern the filter input border turns red, Lib.showCustomAlert() is awaited with the engine error message, and the function returns early so the dialog stays open for correction.
confirmLoad() converted from synchronous to async to support awaiting Lib.showCustomAlert() while the dialog remains in the DOM.

loadTableDataFromDisk() regex try/catch downgraded from a user-facing Lib.showCustomAlert() call to a silent Lib.error() defensive fallback, because a valid pattern is now guaranteed by confirmLoad() for all normal UI-driven flows. Direct programmatic callers are still protected.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Per-subtable clear button (mb-subtable-clear-btn) now dynamically relabels itself: shows 'Clear all COLUMN filters' when only column filters are active (no STF), and 'Clear all filters' when the STF is active (with or without column filters). Achieved by wrapping the button text in a named span (.mb-subtable-clear-btn-label) and updating it in updateFilterButtonsVisibility(). The button title is updated in sync.

In-panel 'Clear all COLUMN filters' button (clearAllStfBtn / .mb-stf-clear-col-btn) inside each STF filter panel: visibility management moved from updateInputStyle() to updateFilterButtonsVisibility(). It now appears only when BOTH the STF input AND at least one column filter are active. Consequence: (a) when only the STF filter is active the button no longer appears, leaving the per-subtable mb-subtable-clear-btn as the sole control; (b) after the user clicks it to clear column filters, runFilter() → updateFilterButtonsVisibility() is called and the button disappears immediately because no column filters remain. The element is given class mb-stf-clear-col-btn so updateFilterButtonsVisibility() can locate it via h3.querySelector('.').

Global 'Clear ALL filters' button (mb-clear-all-filters-btn): condition changed from (globalFilterActive || (stfFiltersActive && columnFiltersActive)) to (globalFilterActive || stfFiltersActive). The button now correctly appears whenever any global or per-subtable (STF) filter is active, not only when both STF and column filters are simultaneously active.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Global 'Clear all COLUMN filters' and 'Clear ALL filters' button visibility: tightened the rules so each button only appears when it is actually useful, avoiding duplicate/redundant controls. Previous behaviour showed both global buttons for any combination of active filters. Corrected rules (global filter aside): (1) STF filter only → neither global clear button is shown; the per-subtable mb-subtable-clear-btn in the h3 controls bar already handles that scope. (2) Column filter(s) only, no STF or global → only 'Clear all COLUMN filters' is shown; 'Clear ALL filters' is superfluous when there is nothing outside the column level to clear. (3) Both STF and column filters active → both 'Clear all COLUMN filters' and 'Clear ALL filters' are shown, because a one-click 'clear everything' action is now genuinely useful. (4) Global filter active (with or without STF/column) → 'Clear ALL filters' is always shown because it is the only button that clears the global field; 'Clear all COLUMN filters' additionally appears if any column filter is also active.

Implemented as a change to the clearAllFiltersBtn condition in updateFilterButtonsVisibility(): from (globalFilterActive || columnFiltersActive || stfFiltersActive) to (globalFilterActive || (stfFiltersActive && columnFiltersActive)). The clearColumnFiltersBtn condition (columnFiltersActive) is unchanged.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Global 'Clear ALL filters' button: was not appearing when only a sub-table (STF) filter was active. The visibility condition in updateFilterButtonsVisibility() was (globalFilterActive || columnFiltersActive) — it included stfFiltersActive in the 'Toggle highlighting' check introduced in 9.97.17 but never added it to the 'Clear ALL filters' check. Fixed by adding stfFiltersActive to the condition so the button now appears whenever any filter at any level (global, sub-table, or column) is active.

Global 'Toggle highlighting' button — second click (restore highlights) did nothing when only a sub-table filter was active. saveFilterHighlightState() only inspected the global filter input and column filter inputs when computing hasContent. Because neither had a value, hasContent was always false for a pure-STF-filter scenario. restoreFilterHighlightState() guards its runFilter() call behind if (!savedFilterHighlights.hasContent) return, so it exited immediately and STF highlights were never restored. Fixed by also querying all .mb-subtable-filter-container input[type="text"] elements in saveFilterHighlightState() and including any non-empty STF values in the stfFilters array and in the hasContent boolean. The existing restoreFilterHighlightState() path (calling runFilter() which calls reapplyAllSubTableFilters()) already handles re-applying STF highlights correctly once hasContent is true.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: In-panel 'Clear ALL COLUMN filters' button (introduced in 9.97.17): corrected behaviour — the button now clears only the column-level filter inputs for that specific sub-table. The STF (sub-table filter) input is intentionally left untouched so the sub-table filter continues to be active while the column filters are wiped. Previously the button also cleared the STF input and restored STF-hidden rows, which was the wrong scope for this action.

Button title updated from 'Clear sub-table filter AND all column filters for this table' to 'Clear all COLUMN filters for this sub-table (sub-table filter is kept)' to accurately describe the corrected behaviour.
Button label updated from 'Clear ALL' to 'Clear all COLUMN filters' to clearly distinguish this in-panel button from the external 'Clear all filters' button in the sub-table controls bar.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Global 'Toggle highlighting' button: visibility is now driven by active filter *strings* rather than DOM highlight span presence. Previously the button was shown only when `.mb-global-filter-highlight` or `.mb-column-filter-highlight` spans existed in the DOM; this caused two failure modes: (1) the button never appeared when only a sub-table (STF) filter was active (those use `.mb-subtable-filter-highlight` spans which were not checked), and (2) after the user toggled highlighting OFF the spans were removed from the DOM, making the button vanish even though filters were still active — leaving no way to re-enable highlighting without retyping the filter. The button now appears whenever any filter string is non-empty (global, any STF, or any column) and disappears as soon as all filter fields are empty.

Global 'Toggle highlighting' button: the column-filter active check now uses `stripColFilterPrefix()` consistently, so a focused-but-empty column filter field carrying only the decorative '🔍 ' prefix is no longer incorrectly counted as an active filter.

sections.label: ✨ Features
items: New in-panel 'Clear ALL' button added to every sub-table filter container (the collapsible panel opened by clicking the 🔍 toggle icon). The button is placed after the Cc/Rx/Ex option row, is initially hidden, and appears as soon as the STF input contains any text. Clicking it: (1) clears the STF input text, (2) calls clearSubFilter() to restore any rows hidden by the STF filter and remove STF highlight spans, (3) clears all column-level filter inputs for that specific table (value, background, and box-shadow), and (4) calls runFilter() so that the global/column filter view is fully refreshed. This gives users a one-click 'reset everything for this sub-table' action while keeping the STF panel open and focused.

sections.label: 🔧 Code Quality
items: updateInputStyle() (inner function of createSubTableFilterContainer) now also controls the visibility of the new in-panel 'Clear ALL' button: shows it when filterInput.value is non-empty, hides it otherwise. Because updateInputStyle() is called by both applySubFilter() and clearSubFilter(), the button's visibility is always in sync with the STF input state without additional event listeners.
createSubTableFilterContainer() @returns JSDoc updated to include the newly exported clearAllStfBtn member.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Column filter ✕ button: clicking the small clear icon now correctly re-focuses the filter field and restores the decorative focus prefix (sa_col_filter_focus_prefix), exactly matching the behaviour of pressing Escape the first time while the field has focus. Previously the browser fired blur before onclick, stripping the prefix before the handler ran, so the field always ended up empty and blurred regardless of previous state.

Sub-table filter — 'Clear all filters' button now also appears when the sub-table (STF) filter input is active (not only when column-level filters are active). Previously the button was driven exclusively by column filter state, so typing in the STF input left the button hidden.
Sub-table filter — 'Toggle highlighting' button now also appears as soon as the STF input receives any text, matching the behaviour that was already in place for column filters.

Sub-table filter — clicking 'Clear all filters' (formerly 'Clear all COLUMN filters') while the STF input is active now also clears the STF field and immediately restores all rows that were hidden by it (data-mb-stf-hidden), removes STF highlight spans, and resets the input border/shadow. Previously only column inputs were cleared, leaving the STF filter visually active with no way to dismiss it via that button.

applySubFilter and clearSubFilter now call updateFilterButtonsVisibility() on completion so the per-subtable 'Clear all filters' and 'Toggle highlighting' buttons update their visibility in real time as the user types or clears the STF field — without requiring a subsequent column-filter interaction or a full runFilter() pass.

sections.label: 🔧 Code Quality
items: createSubTableHighlightButton (standalone helper introduced in 9.97.15) now also calls runFilter() in its ON branch so that column-filter highlights are also restored, not only sub-table-filter highlights.

Per-subtable 'Clear all filters' button: label shortened from 'Clear all COLUMN filters' to 'Clear all filters' and tooltip updated to 'Clear all filters for this sub-table (column filters and sub-table filter)' to reflect the extended scope.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Column filter ✕ button: clicking the clear icon when the field is focused no longer erases the decorative focus prefix. The handler now checks whether the prefix is present before clearing, retaining it and repositioning the cursor so the interaction is identical to pressing Escape once.

Sub-table 'Clear all COLUMN filters' button visibility: the empty-check now uses stripColFilterPrefix() instead of a raw .trim() !== '' test, so a focused-but-empty column filter field (which carries the decorative '🔍 ' prefix) is no longer incorrectly counted as an active filter.

Keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Esc changed to Ctrl+Shift+Esc throughout the script and in ShowAllEntityData_HELP.txt. The previous binding was intercepted by the operating system on several platforms, making the shortcut unreachable.

sections.label: ✨ Features
items: Per-subtable 'Toggle highlighting' button: moved out of the collapsible STF filter panel and into the persistent .mb-subtable-controls bar (next to 'Clear all COLUMN filters') so it remains accessible even when the STF panel is closed. Button starts hidden and is shown/hidden by updateFilterButtonsVisibility() whenever any filter (STF or column) is active for that sub-table.

Per-subtable 'Toggle highlighting' button: the OFF branch now strips both .mb-subtable-filter-highlight AND .mb-column-filter-highlight spans from table rows, not only sub-table filter highlights. The ON branch calls runFilter() (in addition to applySubFilter) to restore column-filter highlights.

New helper function createSubTableHighlightButton(table, categoryName): creates a stateful 'Toggle highlighting' button for the two defensive code-paths in renderGroupedTable where .mb-subtable-controls is rebuilt from scratch (reuse-existing-table branch and missing-controls-during-filtering branch).
createSubTableFilterContainer() JSDoc return type updated to {{ toggleIcon, container, highlightBtn }} to reflect the newly exported highlightBtn.
Ctrl+Shift+Esc shortcut registered in the keyboard shortcuts reference dialog and button tooltip updated accordingly.

sections.label: ✨ Features
items: Column filter prefix guard: while a column filter field has focus, Backspace and Delete cannot consume the decorative focus prefix (sa_col_filter_focus_prefix). The cursor is clamped so it cannot move into the prefix region. ArrowLeft and Shift+ArrowLeft stop at the first character after the prefix. Home / Shift+Home reposition to just after the prefix rather than column 0. Ctrl+A selects only the user-editable text after the prefix.

New keyboard shortcut Shift+Esc: clears all COLUMN filters across all tables (global action — fires regardless of which element currently has focus). Equivalent to clicking the 'Clear all COLUMN filters' button.
New keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Esc: clears ALL filters — global filter, all sub-table filters, and all column filters (global action). Equivalent to clicking the 'Clear ALL filters' button.
Button tooltips updated: 'Clear all COLUMN filters' now shows '— global action (Shift+Esc)' and 'Clear ALL filters' shows '— global action (Ctrl+Esc)' so users discover the shortcuts via hover.
Keyboard shortcuts help dialog: Shift+Esc and Ctrl+Esc entries added to the 'Filter & Search' section.

ShowAllEntityData_HELP.txt updated: new 'COLUMN FILTER FOCUS INDICATORS' section documents the focus prefix, clamping behaviour, and the focus/active background colours. KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS section updated with Shift+Esc, Ctrl+Esc, and an expanded Escape entry. SETTINGS section notes the new colour/prefix settings.

sections.label: 🔧 Code Quality
items: Added guardColFilterPrefixKeydown(input, e) helper function. Handles Backspace, Delete, ArrowLeft, Home, and Ctrl+A via a switch statement with precise selectionStart/selectionEnd arithmetic. Wired as a 'keydown' listener on every .mb-col-filter-input element created by addColumnFilterRow.

Plain-Escape handler narrowed with !e.shiftKey && !e.ctrlKey && !e.metaKey && !e.altKey guard so it never fires when Shift+Esc or Ctrl+Esc are pressed. Shift+Esc and Ctrl+Esc handlers placed before the plain-Escape block and each call stopPropagation() to prevent fall-through.

sections.label: ✨ Features
items: Column filter active-state indicator: when a column filter field loses focus and still contains a filter string, the background colour is now kept (switching from the focus colour to a dedicated 'active' colour, default #fff9c4) rather than cleared. This gives a persistent visual indication of which columns currently have an active filter. The background is only removed on blur when the field is empty.
New configurable setting 'Column Filter Active Background' (sa_col_filter_active_bg, default #fff9c4) added to the Settings Manager in the '🎨 FILTER HIGHLIGHT COLORS' section.

Column filter focus prefix is now configurable: the hard-coded '🔍 ' string has been moved from the module-level constant COL_FILTER_FOCUS_PREFIX into the Settings Manager as 'Column Filter Focus Prefix' (sa_col_filter_focus_prefix, default '🔍 '). It can be set to any string, including empty.

sections.label: 🔧 Code Quality
items: Removed module-level constant COL_FILTER_FOCUS_PREFIX. All three helper functions (stripColFilterPrefix, applyColFilterFocusStyle, removeColFilterFocusStyle) and the Escape-key handler now read Lib.settings.sa_col_filter_focus_prefix at call time (with '🔍 ' as a ?? fallback), so prefix changes in Settings take effect immediately without a page reload.

removeColFilterFocusStyle updated: after stripping the prefix it checks whether the remaining value is empty; if so it clears backgroundColor, otherwise it applies sa_col_filter_active_bg to persist the active-filter visual.

sections.label: ✨ Features
items: Column filter focus indicators: when a column-level filter field receives focus (via Ctrl+C, Tab, or mouse click) a search icon prefix '🔍 ' is prepended to the field and the background turns light yellow (configurable). Both visuals are removed the moment the field loses focus — the stored value is always the clean filter string without any decorative prefix.
New configurable setting 'Column Filter Focus Background' (sa_col_filter_focus_bg, default #fffde7) added to the '🎨 FILTER HIGHLIGHT COLORS' section of the Settings Manager.

Ctrl+C focus behaviour: the cursor is positioned right after the search-icon prefix so the user can start typing immediately. The previous .select() call that selected the entire field content (including the prefix) has been removed.

Escape key handler is now prefix-aware: a column filter field is considered empty when only the focus prefix remains (no actual filter text), so the first Escape press clears the user-entered text but restores the prefix (field stays focused), and the second Escape blurs the field (triggering the blur handler which strips the prefix). The global filter field behaviour is unchanged.

Load from Disk: any column filter inputs still in the DOM when the load is confirmed are explicitly cleared (value and background) before the DOM is rebuilt, guaranteeing no focus-mode artefacts survive into the freshly rendered table.

sections.label: 🔧 Code Quality
items: Added module-level constant COL_FILTER_FOCUS_PREFIX = '🔍 ' as the single source of truth for the decorative prefix string.

Added three helper functions: stripColFilterPrefix(value) strips the prefix before a value is used as a filter string; applyColFilterFocusStyle(input) adds the prefix and bg on focus; removeColFilterFocusStyle(input) strips the prefix and clears the bg on blur.

All code paths that read .mb-col-filter-input values for filtering or status display (getColFilters, runFilter active-count, sub-table filter status, showStatsPanel, saveFilterHighlightState) now call stripColFilterPrefix() so the prefix is never treated as part of the filter expression.

All code paths that programmatically clear column filter inputs (clearAllFilters, clearColumnFiltersBtn onclick, clearSubTableColumnFilters, startFetchingProcess reset loop) now also clear input.style.backgroundColor so no yellow tint lingers after a programmatic clear.

sections.label: 🔧 Code Quality
items: Replaced all fragile querySelector/querySelectorAll UI-element lookups that matched on textContent.includes(), title*=, or placeholder*= with direct document.getElementById() calls — O(1), unambiguous, and immune to label/title text changes.

Added missing id attributes to three buttons that were previously found only by text search: Export button (mb-export-btn), Statistics button (mb-stats-btn), Density button (mb-density-btn). Save to Disk (mb-save-to-disk-btn), Load from Disk (mb-load-from-disk-btn), and the Global Filter input (mb-global-filter-input) already had IDs; their call sites were updated to use getElementById.

Updated call sites: Export existingBtn guard, Export Ctrl+E shortcut, openExportMenu(); Statistics existingBtn guard, Statistics Ctrl+I shortcut (collapsed previous two-step fallback selector into one getElementById call); Density existingBtn guard, Density Ctrl+D shortcut, openDensityMenu(); clearAllFilters() global-filter input clear; Ctrl+G focus-global-filter shortcut; showStatsPanel() filter-status read; saveFilterHighlightState() global-filter read; Ctrl+S save shortcut; Ctrl+L load shortcut; ctrlMFunctionMap 'l' entry.
Escape-key event guard for the global filter changed from e.target.matches('[placeholder*=...]') to e.target.id === 'mb-global-filter-input' for consistency with the new ID-first pattern.

sections.label: 🔧 Code Quality
items: Removed dead-code variable `progressContainer` (assigned null, explicitly noted as removed from controls bar, never referenced).
Removed unused variable `baseUrl` in `startFetchingProcess`: the value was never read — the fetch loop builds URLs via `new URL(window.location.href)` directly.

Removed three dead column-index placeholders `countryDateIdx`, `locationIdx`, and `areaIdx` (declared as -1 and never assigned or read after initialisation; their own comments stated "not used for splitting / idem"). Also removed the only remaining reference to them: a commented-out debug call that referenced `countryDateIdx`.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Settings — 'Global Prefilter Highlight Color' picker opened at black: the schema default was the CSS named color "green". iro.js v5 does not accept CSS named color names as its color initialisation option and silently falls back to #000000, causing the picker wheel to open positioned at black. Fixed by changing the default to "#008000" (the hex equivalent of CSS green) so iro.js always receives a valid hex string. The library (VZ_MBLibrary 3.13.1) also adds a cssColorToHex() normalisation helper as a defensive backstop for any future named-color values.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Load from Disk (re-load on already-rendered page) — sort state not reset: after loading, previously active column sorts (including multi-column sorts with coloured cell tints and highlighted sort-icon buttons) remained visible in the DOM. Fixed by adding a full sort reset in loadTableDataFromDisk() after makeH2sCollapsible(), mirroring the existing filter reset introduced in 9.97.7: (a) clearTints() is called for every entry in multiSortTintRegistry to remove multi-sort cell background colours, (b) .sort-icon-active is stripped from all sort-icon buttons, (c) all per-h3 .mb-sort-status spans are cleared, (d) multiTableSortStates and multiSortTintRegistry are both cleared so stale keys from the previous render do not accumulate.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Load from Disk (re-load on already-rendered page) — filter state not reset: after loading, the global filterInput still held the previous filter value but runFilter() was never called, so newly loaded rows were not affected by the stale filter. 'Toggle highlighting' and 'Clear ALL filters' buttons also showed incorrect visibility. Fixed by clearing filterInput.value, filterStatusDisplay, sortStatusDisplay and calling runFilter() + updateFilterButtonsVisibility() immediately after makeH2sCollapsible().

Load from Disk (re-load on already-rendered page) — h2 click handler broken: makeH2sCollapsible() skipped all h2 elements on the second invocation because they still carried the 'mb-h2-processed' marker from the first render. The h2 DOM nodes are reused across re-renders (only h3s/tables are replaced), so their contentNodes closure and click handler became stale after the first disk load. Fixed by stripping 'mb-h2-processed', 'mb-toggle-h2', the injected toggle icon, and the stored click-handler reference (h2._mbClickHandler / h2._mbToggle) at the top of makeH2sCollapsible() before the registration loop, and by storing each new handler on h2._mbClickHandler so it can be cleanly removed on the next invocation.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: H2 click handler: clicking the plain text label of '.mb-master-toggle' (the 'all Types' text node) now triggers collapse/uncollapse of the current h2 section on plain click, or ALL h2 peers on Ctrl+click — identical to clicking anywhere else on the h2 header. Implemented by detecting 'e.target.classList.contains("mb-master-toggle")' (text-node clicks bubble to the container element, not a child span) and wrapping the '.mb-master-toggle' guard with '!isMasterToggleLabelClick'. The interactive Show/Hide child spans are unaffected — they already call stopPropagation() and never reach this handler.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes / Behaviour Change
items: H2 click handler: '#mb-sort-status-display' now behaves identically to '#mb-filter-status-display' — plain click toggles only the current h2 section; Ctrl+click toggles ALL h2 peers. Both spans are again handled by the single 'isStatusClick' variable (the split into 'isFilterStatusClick'/'isSortStatusClick' introduced in 9.97.4 is reverted as it is no longer needed).

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes / Behaviour Change
items: H2 click handler: corrected toggle semantics for '#mb-filter-status-display' — plain click now toggles only the current h2 section (normal header behaviour); Ctrl+click toggles ALL h2 peers. Previously a plain click on that span incorrectly triggered the all-toggle path. The '#mb-sort-status-display' all-toggle-on-any-click behaviour introduced in 9.97.3 is unchanged. Internally 'isStatusClick' is now split into 'isFilterStatusClick' and 'isSortStatusClick' to allow distinct branching per span.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: H2 click handler: clicking '#mb-sort-status-display' (sort status span) now also triggers collapse/uncollapse of ALL h2 sections, identical to the existing '#mb-filter-status-display' behaviour. Both spans are now detected together as 'isStatusClick' before the guard clause and jointly exempted from the 'filterContainer.contains()' block. The variable formerly named 'isFilterStatusClick' has been renamed to 'isStatusClick' to reflect the broader scope.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: H2 click handler: clicking '#mb-filter-status-display' now correctly triggers collapse/uncollapse of ALL h2 sections — previously the guard clause blocked the click because '#mb-filter-status-display' is a child of 'filterContainer', causing 'filterContainer.contains(e.target)' to return early before 'isFilterStatusClick' was ever evaluated. Fix mirrors the h3 pattern: 'isFilterStatusClick' is now detected before the guard clause, and the 'filterContainer.contains()' check is wrapped with '!isFilterStatusClick' to explicitly exempt the read-only status span.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: H3 headers (multi-table mode): clicking the '.mb-filter-status' or '.mb-sort-status' status text spans inside '.mb-subtable-controls' now also toggles the sub-table collapse/uncollapse — button click functionality is unaffected
H2 headers: clicking the '#mb-filter-status-display' global filter-status span now triggers collapse/uncollapse of ALL h2 sections (same behaviour as Ctrl+Click on the h2 header)

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: H3 row-count badge now shows '(globally_filtered of total)' when only the global filter is active (was showing '(globally_filtered)' without the total)
H3 row-count badge when subtable filter is also active now shows '(locally_filtered of globally_filtered)/total' (was '(locally_filtered of globally_filtered)' without the absolute total)
H2 row-count badge mirrors the same 3-tier format as the sum over all subtables when any subtable filter is active
🔴 Clear ALL filters button now also clears all sub-table specific filter input fields (title updated to 'Clear global/sub-table filters and ALL column filters in all sub-tables')
🎨 Toggle highlighting button now also removes/restores sub-table filter highlights in addition to global and column-filter highlights (title updated accordingly)
Sub-table clear button text changed from 'Clear ALL filters' to 'Clear all COLUMN filters' and title updated to 'Clear all COLUMN filters for this table'

items.text: Sub-table filter: fixed highlight disappearing on second character and rows not restoring when deleting characters
sub: Root cause: applySubFilter() skipped rows with display:none, but those included rows previously hidden by the subtable filter itself — so editing/deleting filter text never re-evaluated them

Fix: applySubFilter() now always restores all stf-hidden rows (data-mb-stf-hidden marker) and clears highlights at the top of every run, then re-evaluates ALL currently-visible rows against the new filter string

Secondary fix: highlight injection now uses a DocumentFragment instead of a wrapper span to inline the highlight spans directly into the parent element, preventing accumulation of empty wrapper spans across re-runs that confused the TreeWalker on subsequent calls
Deleting characters from the filter field now correctly restores matching rows (same behaviour as Escape / clear-button)

items.text: Per-subtable filter UI (🔍 toggle on every h3 header in multi-table mode)

All element IDs are unique per subtable (derived from the sanitized category name, e.g. mb-stf-Album-input)

Filtering is additive: only currently visible rows (not already hidden by global/column filter) are filtered; rows hidden by the subtable filter carry a data-mb-stf-hidden attribute so global re-renders can identify them
Matches are highlighted with .mb-subtable-filter-highlight (green background) independently from global and column filter highlights
🎨 Toggle highlighting button toggles subtable-specific highlight on/off per subtable, independently of the global highlight toggle
First Escape press in the subtable filter input clears the text (and restores hidden rows); second press blurs the input
reapplyAllSubTableFilters() is called at the end of runFilter() so subtable filters remain active after global/column filter re-renders
